title: refinery.ts never calls resetSeedToOpen after merge failure (test-failed / conflict)
description: After merge fails — either test-failed (refinery.ts:423) or conflict (refinery.ts:203) — SQLite run.status is updated correctly but the br bead stays 'closed'. The only recovery is syncBeadStatusOnStartup which runs only on next 'foreman run' invocation. If the user runs 'foreman merge' repeatedly without 'foreman run', the bead drifts indefinitely. Affected lines: refinery.ts:419-437 (test-failed path), refinery.ts:386-401 (conflict path). Fix: call resetSeedToOpen(run.seed_id, this.projectPath) immediately after each status:'test-failed' and status:'conflict' write.

title: no re-enqueue path for failed/conflict merge queue entries
description: When a merge_queue entry transitions to 'failed' or 'conflict', there is no automatic retry or re-enqueue mechanism. The run remains 'completed' in SQLite but is permanently stuck in the queue. The only recovery is manual SQL or workarounds (as seen with dashboard-uv6). Fix: add 'foreman merge --retry' that resets failed/conflict entries back to 'pending' for re-processing. Also consider auto-retry with backoff for transient failures.

title: Archive agent report files to .foreman/reports/<seedId>/ before worktree deletion
description:
Agent pipeline creates valuable report files (EXPLORER_REPORT.md, DEVELOPER_REPORT.md, QA_REPORT.md, REVIEW.md, FINALIZE_REPORT.md, TASK.md) in each worktree. Currently these are gitignored and silently deleted when the worktree is removed. The .gitignore comment already says 'archived to .foreman/reports/ on merge' but this was never implemented.

Implement archiving at two points:
1. refinery.ts — copy all report files to .foreman/reports/<seedId>/ BEFORE removeWorktree() on successful merge
2. agent-worker.ts markStuck() / failure cleanup — same, so failed runs are preserved too

Store per seed: TASK.md, EXPLORER_REPORT.md, QA_REPORT.md, REVIEW.md, FINALIZE_REPORT.md, rotated variants (REVIEW.2026-*.md etc.), plus meta.json with { seedId, runId, mergedAt, branch, verdict }.

Update runs table or add archive_path to SQLite so reports are cross-referenceable. Log archive path in console output during merge/cleanup.
design:
## Implementation Plan

### New module: src/lib/report-archiver.ts

Export one function:
```typescript
export async function archiveReports(
  worktreePath: string,
  seedId: string,
  runId: string,
  projectPath: string,
  meta: { verdict: string; branch: string; phase: string },
): Promise<string | null>  // returns archive dir path or null if nothing archived
```

Logic:
1. Compute archiveDir = join(projectPath, '.foreman', 'reports', seedId)
2. mkdirSync(archiveDir, { recursive: true })
3. Glob for report files in worktreePath: TASK.md, EXPLORER_REPORT*.md, DEVELOPER_REPORT*.md, QA_REPORT*.md, REVIEW*.md, FINALIZE_REPORT*.md (use readdirSync + filter, NOT glob pkg)
4. copyFileSync each found file into archiveDir (overwrite ok)
5. Write meta.json: { seedId, runId, archivedAt, verdict, branch, phase, sourceWorktree: worktreePath }
6. console.error('[report-archiver] Archived N report files to <archiveDir>')
7. Return archiveDir, or null if no files found

Errors: never throw — wrap in try/catch, log warning, return null.

### Modify src/orchestrator/refinery.ts

Import archiveReports. In TWO places where removeWorktree is called:

**Line ~443** (successful merge in mergeCompleted):
```typescript
// Archive before removing
await archiveReports(run.worktree_path, run.seed_id, run.id, this.projectPath, { verdict: 'merged', branch: branchName, phase: 'finalize' });
await removeWorktree(this.projectPath, run.worktree_path);
```

**Line ~570** (resolveConflict theirs strategy):
```typescript
await archiveReports(run.worktree_path, run.seed_id, run.id, this.projectPath, { verdict: 'merged-theirs', branch: branchName, phase: 'finalize' });
await removeWorktree(this.projectPath, run.worktree_path);
```

### Modify src/orchestrator/agent-worker.ts

Import archiveReports. In markStuck() before store.close():
```typescript
// Archive reports so failure context survives worktree deletion
if (config.worktreePath) {
  await archiveReports(config.worktreePath, seedId, runId, config.projectPath ?? process.cwd(), { verdict: stuckStatus, branch: `foreman/${seedId}`, phase });
}
```

Note: markStuck receives worktreePath via config — check WorkerConfig type to confirm the field name.

### Tests: src/lib/__tests__/report-archiver.test.ts

- archives existing report files and writes meta.json
- skips missing files without error  
- never throws when archiveDir creation fails (EACCES)
- returns null when worktreePath has no report files
- meta.json contains expected fields

### Key constraints
- ESM: import with .js extension
- No new dependencies — use node:fs, node:path only
- Non-fatal: all errors caught and logged to stderr
- mkdirSync recursive (not mkdir async) — simpler in sync context

title: Unanchored gitignore patterns silently drop source files matching report-file globs
description: All 7 agent-report gitignore patterns (EXPLORER_REPORT*.md, DEVELOPER_REPORT*.md, QA_REPORT*.md, REVIEW*.md, FINALIZE_REPORT*.md, TASK.md, AGENTS.md) lacked a leading / anchor. In git, an unanchored pattern matches any file at any depth in the tree. On macOS (case-insensitive APFS), REVIEW*.md also matched reviewer-prompt.md in src/orchestrator/templates/ — causing the template file to be silently excluded from every git add. Any agent or developer creating a new source file whose name starts with a report prefix (in any subdirectory) will have it silently dropped from commits with no warning. Fixed: added leading / to anchor all patterns to repo root.
close_reason: Fixed: added leading / to all 7 agent-report gitignore patterns in .gitignore, anchoring them to repo root. Also removed now-unnecessary !src/orchestrator/templates/*.md negation exception.

title: br doctor --repair does not clean up .br_recovery/ artifacts after successful recovery
description: After a crash-and-recovery event, br preserves backup files in .beads/.br_recovery/ (e.g. beads.db.*.bak, beads.db-shm.*.bak, beads.db-wal.*.bak). br doctor correctly reports these as WARN db.recovery_artifacts, but running br doctor --repair does not remove them — it only acts on ERRORs, leaving the warning unresolved. The artifacts accumulate indefinitely and must be removed manually. Expected: --repair (or a dedicated --clean-recovery flag) should remove stale .bak files from .br_recovery/ once integrity_check and counts.db_vs_jsonl both pass OK, confirming the recovery succeeded.

title: Auto-dispatch new beads to agents when capacity available
description: When a new bead is added (via 'br create'), foreman should detect it and automatically spawn a new agent worker if activeAgents < maxAgents. Currently foreman only dispatches when 'foreman run' is explicitly called. The watch loop re-dispatches after each wave but doesn't react to new beads added mid-session. Feature should: 1) Monitor .beads/beads.jsonl for changes (file watch or poll), 2) When a new ready issue appears, call dispatcher if activeAgents < maxAgents, 3) Work in --watch mode. Enables true continuous-dispatch where work starts immediately when a bead is created.

title: merge queue has no continuous processing loop — completed work waits indefinitely
description: foreman merge is a manual one-shot command with no background polling. Once agents complete and enqueue their branches, the work just sits in merge_queue with status='pending' until the user remembers to run foreman merge. This is especially painful with the daemon mode planned in bd-wc91 — if foreman run runs continuously, merge should too. Fix: add a --watch flag to foreman merge (or integrate merge polling into the foreman run daemon loop) that continuously processes pending merge_queue entries as they appear.

description: When bead status drifts between br and SQLite (after crashes, token exhaustion, manual resets), there is no automatic reconciliation. Add a startup reconciliation step to 'foreman run': 1) Query SQLite for runs in running/completed/failed/stuck status, 2) For each run, check br bead status, 3) If SQLite=running but br=open → call br update in_progress, 4) If SQLite=completed but br=in_progress → call br close, 5) If SQLite=failed/stuck but br=in_progress → call br update open. Run this before the dispatch loop. Also expose as 'foreman doctor --fix' action.

title: dispatcher: no backoff between retries when a seed repeatedly goes stuck
description:
When a seed is reset to open after a stuck run, the dispatcher re-dispatches it on the next cycle with no delay or backoff. For deterministic failures (e.g. non-fast-forward push), this creates a tight retry loop.

bd-qtqs accumulated 151 stuck runs in ~20 minutes — roughly one retry every 7-8 seconds.

The sentinel/dispatcher should track recent stuck counts per seed and apply exponential backoff (or max retry count) before re-dispatching. The merge queue already has RETRY_CONFIG with maxRetries=3 and exponential backoff — similar logic is needed in the dispatch path for stuck seeds.
notes: Merge skipped: unresolved conflict markers in src/orchestrator/refinery.ts, src/orchestrator/__tests__/refinery-conflict-scan.test.ts, src/orchestrator/__tests__/merge-validator.test.ts, src/orchestrator/__tests__/conflict-resolver-t3.test.ts. title: Pipeline finalize phase never flushes JSONL after br close
description: closeSeed() and resetSeedToOpen() call br close/update via execFileSync subprocess, but neither agent-worker.ts finalize phase nor refinery.ts ever calls 'br sync --flush-only' afterward. Result: issues.jsonl is always stale after a pipeline run — beads show as in_progress in the JSONL even though SQLite has them closed. Fix: call execBr(['sync', '--flush-only'], projectPath) at the end of the finalize phase in agent-worker.ts, and after bead closes in refinery.ts (post-merge and post-PR-create).

title: bd-x2fp bug scope was too narrow: closeSeed and resetSeedToOpen had same execBr dirty-flag bug
description: The original bd-x2fp bug report described the dirty-flag issue only for syncBeadStatusOnStartup. Investigation during fix revealed that closeSeed and resetSeedToOpen also called execBr(['sync','--flush-only']) for their flush steps — both were affected by the same silent no-op bug. All three were fixed in cf2464b. Should be noted in retrospective: when fixing a pattern bug, search all callers of the affected pattern before closing.

NOTE: The previous pipeline run falsely closed this bead after only adding a stub selectBackend() method. The actual routing logic was never implemented. This description overrides the previous false close — implement the full routing as described below.

title: sentinel: duplicate runs + merge_queue entries when task already completed
description: The sentinel fires on an interval and checks all seeds. If the sentinel processes a seed that already has a completed run (or is mid-merge), it creates additional 'completed' runs rows and merge_queue 'pending' rows. This caused bd-ybs8 to accumulate 92 runs rows and 91 merge_queue entries, causing foreman merge to loop indefinitely processing the same seed. Fix: before creating a new completed run or enqueuing a merge, check if a run with status='completed'/'merged' already exists for the seed_id and skip if so.
close_reason: Fixed: reconcile() now deduplicates by seed_id in addition to run_id, preventing sentinel-spawned duplicate queue entries

title: Load agent phase prompts from templates/ markdown files instead of hardcoded TypeScript
description: src/orchestrator/roles.ts contains 5 hardcoded prompt functions (explorerPrompt, developerPrompt, qaPrompt, reviewerPrompt, sentinelPrompt) as TypeScript template strings. Any prompt tuning requires a TypeScript edit and rebuild. Fix: create a templates/phases/ directory with markdown files for each phase and render them at runtime with placeholder substitution. File structure: templates/phases/explorer.md, developer.md, qa.md, reviewer.md, sentinel.md, finalize.md and templates/task.md (replacing workerAgentMd() in src/orchestrator/templates.ts). Use {{SEED_ID}}, {{TITLE}}, {{DESCRIPTION}}, {{WORKTREE_PATH}} as placeholders. Load order: (1) check .foreman/templates/<phase>.md for project-local overrides, (2) fall back to templates/phases/<phase>.md in the repo, (3) fall back to hardcoded default in roles.ts. This allows prompt tuning without TypeScript changes, makes prompt history readable in git diffs, and enables per-project customization. Implement a loadTemplate(phase, vars, projectPath?) helper in src/orchestrator/templates.ts that handles resolution and substitution.

title: QA sentinel: continuous testing agent for main/master branch
description:
Add a new foreman command (e.g. 'foreman qa' or 'foreman sentinel') that spawns a long-running QA agent which:

1. Continuously (or on a schedule/watch) runs the project test suite against the current main/master branch
2. On test failure, automatically creates a beads bug via 'br create --type bug' with:
   - Title summarising the failing test/suite
   - Description containing the full failure output, stack trace, and affected files
   - Priority derived from failure severity (P1 for regressions, P2 for pre-existing)
3. De-duplicates: checks for an open bead with the same test name before creating a duplicate
4. On test recovery (previously failing test now passes), closes the corresponding bead
5. Supports --interval <seconds> flag to control polling frequency (default 300s / 5 min)
6. Respects --no-auto-open to skip bead creation (dry-run mode)

The agent should be implemented as a new CLI command in src/cli/commands/qa-sentinel.ts (or similar), reusing the existing br wrapper (src/lib/beads-rust.ts) for bead creation and the store for dedup tracking.

Acceptance criteria:
- 'foreman sentinel --once' runs tests once, logs any failures as bugs, exits
- 'foreman sentinel --watch' runs continuously at configurable interval
- Created beads are tagged with kind:qa-regression label
- Existing open regression bead for same test is not duplicated
- Passing tests auto-close their corresponding regression beads

title: TRD: Tmux-Based Session Attachment
description:
**Document ID:** TRD-ATTACH-SESSION
**Version:** 1.1
**Created:** 2026-03-13
**Last Updated:** 2026-03-13
**PRD Reference:** PRD-ATTACH-SESSION v1.1
**Status:** Implementation Ready

## Quality Requirements
### 6.1 Testing Standards

| Type | Target | Notes |
|------|--------|-------|
| Unit test coverage | >= 80% | All new modules must have co-located `__tests__/`. Mock `execFile` calls to tmux binary. |
| Integration test coverage | >= 70% | Tmux integration, fallback paths, E2E dispatch-attach cycle |
| Real tmux tests | CI-aware | Integration tests use real tmux sessions. Use `describe.skipIf(!tmuxAvailable)` to skip on CI environments where tmux is not installed. |
| Test framework | Vitest | Co-located in `__tests__/` subdirectories per CLAUDE.md |

### 6.2 Code Quality

- TypeScript strict mode -- no `any` escape hatches
- ESM only -- all imports use `.js` extensions
- TDD methodology -- RED-GREEN-REFACTOR for all coding tasks
- Non-interactive commands -- all shell commands must use `-f` flags (no `-i` prompts)
- Input validation at boundaries only (CLI argument parsing)

### 6.3 Performance Targets

| Operation | Target | Task Reference |
|-----------|--------|----------------|
| tmux session creation overhead | < 200ms | AT-T003, AT-T013 |
| Follow mode latency (output to display) | < 2s | AT-T020 |
| Zombie detection speed | < 30s from death | AT-T031 |
| Session listing render | < 500ms for 20 sessions | AT-T024 |
| tmux availability check (cached) | < 1ms after first call | AT-T001 |

### 6.4 Compatibility

- Works with tmux >= 3.0 (macOS Homebrew default, Ubuntu 20.04+)

Refactor `spawnWorkerProcess()` in dispatcher.ts to use a `SpawnStrategy` interface pattern. Define `SpawnStrategy` interface with `spawn(config: WorkerConfig): Promise<{ tmuxSession?: string }>`. Implement `TmuxSpawnStrategy` (calls `TmuxClient.createSession()`) and `DetachedSpawnStrategy` (existing `spawn(tsx, [...], { detached: true })` path). The tmux command string must be: `<tsxBin> <workerScript> <configPath> > <outLog> 2> <errLog>`. Pass `config.worktreePath` as `cwd`. title: foreman stop: graceful shutdown command for running foreman processes
description:
Add a 'foreman stop' command (and supporting infrastructure) to reliably stop a running foreman instance.

## Problem
'foreman run --watch' (and the upcoming 'foreman sentinel --watch') run continuously with no reliable way to stop them other than Ctrl+C or kill. When running detached or in a background terminal, there is no clean shutdown path.

## Proposed Solution

### PID file approach
- On startup, 'foreman run' and 'foreman sentinel' write a PID file to .foreman/foreman.pid (or .foreman/<command>.pid for multiple instances)
- 'foreman stop' reads the PID file and sends SIGTERM; waits up to 10s for clean exit, then SIGKILL
- PID file is removed on clean shutdown (via process signal handlers)

### New CLI command: foreman stop
- 'foreman stop' — stops the default running foreman process
- 'foreman stop --all' — stops all running foreman processes (run + sentinel)
- 'foreman stop --command run|sentinel' — targets a specific command
- Exit codes: 0 = stopped, 1 = no process found, 2 = kill failed

### Signal handling in foreman run / sentinel
- Register SIGTERM/SIGINT handlers that:
  1. Stop dispatching new tasks
  2. Wait for in-progress agent runs to reach a safe checkpoint (or timeout after 30s)
  3. Remove PID file
  4. Exit cleanly with code 0

### foreman status enhancement
- Show whether foreman is currently running (reads PID file, checks process liveness)

## Acceptance Criteria
- 'foreman stop' terminates a background 'foreman run --watch' process cleanly
- In-flight agent runs are not killed mid-phase; dispatch loop stops accepting new work
- PID file is cleaned up on both clean exit and SIGKILL fallback
- 'foreman status' indicates running/stopped state
- Works on macOS and Linux

title: Add --dry-run to worktree clean command
description: 'foreman worktree clean' removes worktrees for completed/merged/failed runs but has no --dry-run flag, unlike every other destructive command in foreman (reset, stop, merge, decompose, doctor, sling). Add --dry-run to 'worktree clean' that lists what would be removed without actually removing it. Consistent with the rest of the CLI.

title: Pipeline agents falsely close beads without verifying implementation actually landed in the codebase
description: Multiple beads (bd-brsn, bd-a3go, others) have been marked 'Completed via pipeline' by worker agents but the actual implementation is absent from the codebase. The agent closes the seed via 'br close' in the finalize phase without any check that the work it was asked to do actually exists. Root causes: (1) The finalize phase only runs 'npx tsc --noEmit' (type check) — passing type check does not verify the feature was implemented. (2) The agent may have committed stub/skeleton code that satisfies the type checker but not the bead requirements. (3) There is no acceptance criteria check — the finalize phase has no step that re-reads the bead description and confirms the implementation matches. Fix: finalize() should include a verification step that: reads the bead description, checks for expected files/functions mentioned in the description, and only calls br close if the verification passes. Alternatively, add a post-merge integration test gate that re-opens the bead if the feature is not present.

title: CLAUDE.md in worktrees contains no sessionlog requirement — agents have no direction to log sessions
description: The CLAUDE.md present in all worktrees (copied from the project root at worktree creation time) contains detailed br workflow instructions and session protocol, but has no mention of SessionLogs/ or /ensemble:sessionlog. Even if an interactive Claude Code agent read CLAUDE.md in a worktree, it would receive no instruction to create a session log. The session protocol section ends at br sync --flush-only with no sessionlog step. Adding a sessionlog instruction to CLAUDE.md would only help for manually-attached interactive sessions, not pipeline SDK workers (see bd-p4y7), but is still a gap for the former case.

title: Add --json output to status, monitor, and merge --list
description: --json flag is available on doctor, merge --stats, worktree list, sentinel status, and sling trd but missing from status, monitor, and merge --list — all natural candidates for scripting and automation. Add --json flag to: 1) foreman status (output task counts + active runs as JSON), 2) foreman monitor (output run health check as JSON), 3) foreman merge --list (output merge queue entries as JSON).

title: Remove or repurpose --no-auto-merge flag from foreman run
description: --no-auto-merge disables per-dispatch merge queue processing. With MergeAgentDaemon running as a continuous daemon (started by foreman run alongside sentinel), merge is no longer triggered per-dispatch. The flag is misleading — the daemon runs regardless. Remove the flag and update docs to explain merge is now always-on via the daemon.

title: Remove foreman merge command
description: The MergeAgentDaemon now handles merges automatically via Agent Mail branch-ready messages. The manual foreman merge command is superseded. Remove src/cli/commands/merge.ts and deregister it from the CLI.

title: Merge failures do not update bead status — status mismatch until next foreman startup
description:
When a merge fails (conflict, test-failed, PR creation error, exception), the run status in SQLite is updated but the bead in br is never touched — it stays 'closed' or whatever it was. syncBeadStatusOnStartup() repairs this on next restart but there is an immediate mismatch.

The correct behavior by failure type:
- Merge conflict → set bead to 'blocked' + comment explaining the conflict
- Test failure → set bead to 'blocked' + comment with test output summary  
- PR creation failure → set bead to 'blocked' + comment with error
- Unexpected exception → set bead to 'failed' + comment

Fix: merge.ts should update the bead status immediately on any merge failure rather than waiting for syncBeadStatusOnStartup() on next restart.

title: Remove --attach/--no-attach flags from foreman run
description: The --attach and --no-attach flags auto-attach to a tmux session after dispatch. PiRpcSpawnStrategy uses RPC over stdin/stdout — no tmux session exists. Remove these flags from src/cli/commands/run.ts once PiRpcSpawnStrategy is the default spawn strategy.

title: doctor: zombie run detection is broken for SDK-based agent workers
description: In src/orchestrator/doctor.ts, extractPid() uses regex /pid-(\d+)/ to extract a PID from the session_key. However, SDK-based agent workers use session_key format 'foreman:sdk:<model>:<uuid>' which contains no PID. extractPid() returns null for all SDK runs, so isProcessAlive() is called with null which makes isAlive=false, causing ALL SDK-based running runs to be reported as zombies — even when live agent-worker.ts processes are actively running them. The fix must detect liveness differently for SDK runners, e.g. by checking for a running agent-worker.ts process whose worker JSON file contains the run ID, or by storing the actual PID in a separate column.

title: doctor: checkFailedStuckRuns has no --fix path; 155 failed runs accumulate indefinitely
description:
checkFailedStuckRuns() in doctor.ts:496 only warns about failed/stuck runs but has no fix logic — it takes no opts parameter and never cleans anything up. Running 'foreman doctor --fix' leaves the warning unchanged.

After the bd-qtqs retry loop bug (bd-zwtr), 155 failed runs accumulated in the DB. doctor --fix cannot remove them. They persist indefinitely and noise the doctor output.

Two fixes needed:
1. Add fix option to checkFailedStuckRuns() to purge/archive failed runs older than N days (e.g. 7 days) for seeds that are now closed or have a successful later run.
2. Or: filter getRunsByStatus('failed') to exclude runs where the seed has a subsequent 'completed' or 'merged' run — these are historical retries, not actionable failures.

The check should also distinguish between:
- Seeds with ONLY failed runs (actionable — needs attention)
- Seeds with failed runs BUT ALSO a later completed/merged run (noise — historical retries)

title: Rename 'seed' terminology to 'bead' throughout codebase
description:
The codebase inconsistently uses 'seed' and 'bead' to refer to the same concept (a br/beads task ID). The user-facing CLI uses --seed flags and 'seed_id' internally, but the underlying task tracker is called 'beads' (br). This creates confusing UX and inconsistent naming.

## Scope of Changes

### CLI flags
- foreman run --seed <id>        → foreman run --bead <id>
- foreman merge --seed <id>      → foreman merge --bead <id>
- foreman reset --seed <id>      → foreman reset --bead <id>
- foreman status --seed <id>     → foreman status --bead <id>
- Any other --seed flags in CLI commands

### Internal naming (TypeScript)
- seed_id column references in SQLite queries → bead_id (or rename column via migration)
- Run.seed_id field → Run.bead_id
- opts.seed → opts.bead
- seedId variables → beadId
- getRunsByStatus / getRunsBySeed → update param names
- MergeQueueEntry.seed_id → bead_id
- mergeCompleted({ seedId }) → mergeCompleted({ beadId })
- getCompletedRuns(projectId, seedId) → getCompletedRuns(projectId, beadId)
- resetForRetry(seedId) → resetForRetry(beadId)
- All other internal seed_id / seedId references

### Worker config / agent-worker
- WorkerConfig.seed_id → WorkerConfig.bead_id
- All references in dispatcher.ts, agent-worker.ts, monitor.ts

### Store schema migration
- Add SQLite migration: ALTER TABLE runs RENAME COLUMN seed_id TO bead_id
- Add SQLite migration: ALTER TABLE merge_queue RENAME COLUMN seed_id TO bead_id
- Ensure migration runs on first open of existing databases

### Tests
- Update all test fixtures, mocks, and assertions that reference seed_id / seedId / opts.seed

### Backwards compatibility
- Accept --seed as a deprecated alias for --bead with a deprecation warning during a transition period

## Acceptance Criteria
- foreman run --bead <id> dispatches the correct task
- foreman merge --bead <id> merges the correct branch
- Internal code uses beadId/bead_id consistently
- All existing tests pass with updated naming
- --seed still works but prints deprecation warning

title: Move bead lifecycle ownership to agent-worker — eliminate dispatcher/worker split
description: Currently bead status management is split: dispatcher.ts marks in_progress at line 186, agent-worker.ts finalizes via br close, and reset.ts resets to open. This causes race conditions (bd-ng9) and missing updates (bd-7wa). Consolidate: 1) Remove seeds.update(in_progress) from dispatcher.ts — instead pass seeds/br client config to agent-worker via WorkerConfig, 2) agent-worker marks in_progress when starting (before explorer phase), 3) agent-worker resets to open on failure/stuck (currently it only updates SQLite), 4) agent-worker calls br close on success (already does this in finalize). This makes agent-worker the single owner of bead lifecycle, eliminating the race condition.

title: [trd:seeds-to-br-bv-migration:phase:4] Sprint 4 — Phase 4: Cleanup
description: Phase 4 of TRD: Migrate Task Management from seeds (sd) to br + bv. Contains 10 tasks. Goal: Remove all seeds/sd infrastructure, feature flags, and deprecated code.

title: Merge failures never add a comment to the bead explaining what went wrong
description:
When a merge fails for any reason (test failures, rebase conflict, code conflict, PR error), no comment is written to the bead. The failure reason is stored in SQLite merge_queue.error and the events table, but is completely invisible to a user checking 'br show <seedId>'.

The correct behavior:
- All merge failures should call 'br comment add <seedId> "Merge failed (<type>): <reason>"'
- For test failures: include the failing test names/output (truncated)
- For conflicts: include the conflicting files
- For PR errors: include the API error message

This applies to all failure types: test-failed, conflict, pr-creation-failed, exception. Without this, the bead shows stale status with no audit trail of what went wrong.

The mock is never triggered and mockExecBr is no longer checked in any test that is actually exercising the real code path. Should be cleaned up to avoid confusion.

title: Worktrees missing node_modules: npm install never runs after createWorktree()
description: When foreman creates a git worktree via createWorktree(), the new worktree directory does NOT get node_modules populated. git worktree add shares the .git dir but does NOT symlink or copy node_modules. Worker agents then fail when they try to run tsx, npx tsc, vitest, or any node binary because node_modules/.bin/* does not exist. This was observed when foreman doctor tests failed with ENOENT on node_modules/.bin/tsx — fixed only by manually running npm install. Fix: dispatcher or createWorktree() should run 'npm install --prefer-offline' (or create a symlink to the main repo node_modules) immediately after the worktree is created, before spawning the agent.

comments.author: ldangelo
comments.text: Likely fix: symlink node_modules from the main repo into each worktree immediately after createWorktree() returns. Since all worktrees share the same package.json, a symlink is correct and fast — no reinstall needed. Alternative is 'npm install --prefer-offline' but that's slower and redundant. The symlink approach: ln -s <projectRoot>/node_modules <worktreePath>/node_modules

title: agent crash between git push and enqueue loses branch silently
description: If agent-worker crashes (OOM, token exhaustion, host sleep) after a successful git push but before enqueueToMergeQueue() writes to SQLite, the push happened but no merge_queue entry exists. On recovery, foreman reset resets the run to 'open' and the agent re-runs from scratch, potentially pushing a duplicate branch. The pushed branch is orphaned with no tracking in SQLite. Fix: write the merge_queue entry BEFORE calling git push (with status='pending'), then update to 'ready' after push succeeds. This makes the queue entry the source of truth rather than a post-hoc write.

title: Race condition: foreman reset resets bead status after auto-dispatch marks in_progress
description: foreman run --watch has an auto-dispatch callback that fires when runs change state (e.g., when reset marks runs as failed). Sequence: 1) foreman reset marks runs failed + beads open, 2) auto-dispatch fires, marks beads in_progress, spawns agents, 3) foreman reset (still executing) calls br update <id> --status=open AFTER auto-dispatch set them in_progress. Result: agents running in SQLite as running, br bead shows open. Fix: in reset.ts, only reset bead status to open if there is NO new running run for that seed_id in SQLite after the reset completes. Check store.getRunsByStatus('running') for the seed before calling seeds.update(id, { status: 'open' }).

title: No command to purge zombie failed run records for already-closed beads
description: When a bead succeeds after multiple failed attempts (common during token exhaustion/restart cycles), the old failed run records accumulate in SQLite with no clean removal path. foreman doctor warns about them but foreman reset is unsafe (it reopens closed beads — see related bug). There is no 'foreman reset --purge' or 'foreman doctor --fix' path to delete zombie run records where the bead is already closed. Users must resort to raw SQL. Fix: add a 'foreman reset --purge-closed' flag (or 'foreman doctor --fix' action) that DELETEs failed run records whose seed bead status is already 'closed', without touching bead state. Should also prune orphaned worktrees/branches for those runs.

title: foreman merge misses agents that finish after merge was invoked
description: foreman merge is a one-shot command that reads pending merge_queue entries at the moment it runs. Any agent-worker that finishes and enqueues AFTER foreman merge was invoked is not picked up until the user manually runs foreman merge again. With multiple long-running agents, this is common. Fix: foreman merge --watch (or the daemon mode from bd-wc91) should poll for new pending merge_queue entries and process them automatically, similar to how foreman run watches for new beads.

title: Add pipeline phase labels to beads as agents progress through phases
description: agent-worker.ts runs 5 phases: explorer, developer, qa, reviewer, finalize. Currently br only sees open→in_progress→closed with no visibility into which phase is active. Add br label updates between phases: 1) Before explorer: br update <id> --labels phase:exploring, 2) Before developer: phase:developing, 3) Before qa: phase:testing, 4) Before reviewer: phase:reviewing, 5) Before finalize: phase:finalizing, 6) After merge (in refinery): phase:merged. Use existing br update --labels mechanism. Labels are additive so old phase labels stay (breadcrumb trail). This lets 'br show <id>' show full pipeline progress without needing foreman status/dashboard.

title: Consolidate monitor command: fold stuck-detection into reset, deprecate monitor
description: The 'foreman monitor' command overlaps significantly with 'foreman status' and 'foreman dashboard'. Proposal: 1) Move stuck-run detection logic from monitor into 'foreman reset' (add --auto or --monitor flag to reset that runs the health-check loop), 2) Remove 'foreman monitor' command from CLI, 3) Update 'foreman reset --help' to document the health-check behavior, 4) Keep 'foreman status' (quick snapshot) and 'foreman dashboard' (live TUI) as-is. This reduces command surface area and makes each command's purpose unambiguous.

title: finalize() runs 'npx tsc --noEmit' without first running npm install or npm run build
description: The finalize() phase in agent-worker.ts calls execFileSync('npx', ['tsc', '--noEmit']) to type-check the worktree before committing. However: (1) npm install is never run in the worktree so npx may not be available or may use a different TypeScript version. (2) There is no 'npm run build' step to verify the compiled output actually works — only the type check is run. If the worktree has no node_modules (see bd-n2c6), the tsc call silently fails or errors, but finalize() continues anyway (type check failure is non-fatal). The QA phase also runs 'npm test' which requires node_modules. All pipeline phases that invoke npm/npx commands against the worktree are potentially broken without a prior npm install step.

title: ensemble:sessionlog skill is a human-invoked Claude Code command, not reachable from SDK query()
description: The ensemble:sessionlog skill is defined at ~/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/ensemble/packages/core/commands/sessionlog.yaml and is only available as a slash command (/ensemble:sessionlog) in an interactive Claude Code terminal session. SDK query() calls made by agent-worker.ts runPhase() do not load user-level Claude Code plugins/skills. Even if a prompt instructed a phase agent to 'run /ensemble:sessionlog', the SDK agent would not have access to the skill definition and could not execute it. A different mechanism is needed — either a TypeScript finalize step that writes a SessionLogs entry directly, or passing the sessionlog template as inline instructions in the phase prompt.

title: auto-merge fires after watchRunsInk — completed branches wait for running agents
description: In run.ts dispatch loop, autoMerge() is called AFTER watchRunsInk() returns. watchRunsInk blocks until the active agent completes. Completed branches sit unmerged for hours while a single long-running agent occupies the watch. Fix: call autoMerge() BEFORE watchRunsInk so already-completed work merges immediately.
design:
## Fix in src/cli/commands/run.ts

Current order in the 'nothing dispatched, agents running' branch:
  1. watchRunsInk(activeRunIds)  -- blocks until agent done
  2. autoMerge()                 -- only fires after agent finishes

Correct order:
  1. autoMerge()                 -- merge completed work immediately  
  2. watchRunsInk(activeRunIds)  -- then watch still-running agents
  3. autoMerge()                 -- merge the agent that just finished

### Change

Before the 'const activeRuns = store.getActiveRuns()' line, insert a first autoMerge call (same try/catch pattern as the existing one).

Keep the existing autoMerge call after watchRunsInk as-is — the agent that just finished also needs merging. autoMerge() is idempotent.

### Tests in src/cli/__tests__/run-auto-merge.test.ts

Add: autoMerge called before watchRunsInk when pre-existing completed runs exist

title: finalize() runs git add -A with no detection of silently-ignored new files
description: The finalize phase in src/orchestrator/agent-worker.ts runs execFileSync('git', ['add', '-A']) then immediately commits. There is no git check-ignore, git status --ignored, or inspection of stderr for 'The following paths are ignored' warnings. If an agent creates a new source file whose name matches any gitignore pattern, git add -A silently skips it, the commit proceeds without the file, and the pipeline reports SUCCESS. The missing file is permanently lost with no error, no log entry, and no indication in FINALIZE_REPORT.md. Root cause of bd-brsn taking multiple merge attempts: reviewer-prompt.md was silently ignored.

title: buildSpawnPrompt() explicit completion steps override worker-agent.md sessionlog instruction
description: dispatcher.ts buildSpawnPrompt() gives Claude an explicit 4-step completion sequence (br close, git add, git commit, git push) that does NOT include /ensemble:sessionlog. Even though worker-agent.md lists sessionlog in step 6 and the quality checklist, the spawn prompt's explicit instructions take precedence and Claude follows them without running sessionlog. Fix: add '/ensemble:sessionlog' as a step in buildSpawnPrompt() before the git close/commit/push sequence, e.g.: 'Run /ensemble:sessionlog to save a session transcript, then when completely finished: br close...'.

title: markStuck() resets bead to open but never adds a comment explaining the failure reason
description:
When a pipeline phase fails, markStuck() calls resetSeedToOpen() which resets the bead to 'open', but never adds a comment explaining the failure reason. A user checking 'br show <seedId>' has no way to know why the task failed or which phase failed — the reason is trapped in SQLite/logs only.

The correct behavior depends on failure type:
- Transient errors (rate limit, timeout) → reset to 'open' so it retries automatically, add comment with reason
- Permanent failures (SDK error, max retries exceeded) → set to 'failed' + comment with phase name and error summary

Fix: after resetSeedToOpen() or on permanent failure, call 'br comment add <seedId> <reason>' with the phase name and error summary. Use 'br update <seedId> --status failed' for permanent failures instead of resetting to open.

title: Migrate ForemanStore to project-local storage
description:
Currently ForemanStore() with no argument opens ~/.foreman/foreman.db — a single global SQLite database shared across all projects. This causes foreman merge --list to show runs and queue entries from every project, and makes it impossible to have isolated state per-repo.

## Goal
Store the database at <projectRoot>/.foreman/foreman.db (local-first). Add .foreman/ to .gitignore.

## Changes Required

### src/lib/store.ts
- Change default dbPath from ~/.foreman/foreman.db to .foreman/foreman.db relative to the project root
- Add a static ForemanStore.forProject(projectPath: string) factory that resolves <projectPath>/.foreman/foreman.db
- Keep the explicit dbPath constructor arg for tests (already uses :memory: or tmpDir)

### src/cli/commands/ (all commands)
- Pass projectPath (from getRepoRoot(process.cwd())) to ForemanStore.forProject() instead of new ForemanStore()
- Affected: merge.ts, run.ts, status.ts, monitor.ts, reset.ts, init.ts, dashboard.ts, pr.ts, doctor.ts, attach.ts, worktree.ts, plan.ts

### src/orchestrator/agent-worker.ts
- WorkerConfig already has projectPath — pass it to ForemanStore.forProject(projectPath)

### Migration on foreman init
- If ~/.foreman/foreman.db exists and <projectRoot>/.foreman/foreman.db does not, offer to migrate runs for this project from the global db

### MergeQueue project isolation (bonus)
- Add project_id column filter to MergeQueue.list() and MergeQueue.dequeue() so even with a shared db, only the current project's queue entries are returned

### .gitignore
- Add .foreman/ to the project .gitignore template used by foreman init

## Acceptance Criteria
- foreman merge --list from /project-a only shows project-a queue entries
- foreman merge --list from /project-b only shows project-b queue entries
- Existing tests pass (they use explicit dbPath already)
- New unit test: two ForemanStore instances for different project paths open different db files

title: Pipeline agent-worker never invokes sessionlog — no SessionLogs/ produced in worktrees
description: The agent-worker pipeline (agent-worker.ts runPipeline) orchestrates Explorer→Developer→QA→Reviewer→Finalize phases entirely in TypeScript. None of the SDK query() calls include a sessionlog instruction, and there is no post-pipeline step that calls /ensemble:sessionlog or any equivalent. The finalize() function only runs git add/commit/push and br close. Result: every bd-* worktree produced by foreman run --pipeline has no SessionLogs/ directory. Only old worktrees (foreman-56aa, bd-0tl4) that pre-date pipeline mode contain session logs, because those were created by manual Claude Code interactive sessions where /ensemble:sessionlog was invoked by hand.

title: Bead closed before merge — fundamental lifecycle flaw
description:
closeSeed() in agent-worker.ts:623 runs the moment the pipeline finishes (finalize phase), long before foreman merge ever runs. The bead shows 'closed' in br while run.status='completed' sits unmerged for minutes/hours/indefinitely. If merge later fails (test-failed, conflict), the bead stays permanently closed with the branch never on main. This is the root cause of all 'bead closed but worktree unmerged' reports.

Root cause: bead lifecycle is tied to pipeline completion, not to branch landing on main.
design:
## The Correct Lifecycle

Change the bead lifecycle to match the branch lifecycle:

  open → in_progress → [review] → closed

Where 'review' means 'pipeline done, branch pushed, awaiting merge'.

## Step 1 — Use 'review' status in finalize() instead of closing

In agent-worker.ts finalize(), replace the closeSeed() call at line 623:

  // Before: closes the bead immediately
  await closeSeed(seedId, config.projectPath);

  // After: set bead to 'review' — pipeline done, pending merge
  await this.seeds.update(seedId, { status: 'review' });

If seeds client is not available in agent-worker context, use the beads-rust client directly:
  execFileSync(brPath(), ['update', seedId, '--status', 'review'], execOpts(projectPath));

## Step 2 — Close bead in refinery.ts after successful merge

In refinery.ts, after store.updateRun(run.id, { status: 'merged' }) at line 449, add:

  import { closeSeed, resetSeedToOpen } from '../orchestrator/task-backend-ops.js';
  ...
  this.store.updateRun(run.id, { status: 'merged', completed_at: new Date().toISOString() });
  await closeSeed(run.seed_id, this.projectPath);  // NOW the bead closes — branch is on main
  console.error('[refinery] Closed bead %s after successful merge', run.seed_id);

## Step 3 — resetSeedToOpen in refinery.ts after test-failed / conflict

After store.updateRun(run.id, { status: 'test-failed' }) at line 423:
  await resetSeedToOpen(run.seed_id, this.projectPath);
  console.error('[refinery] Reset bead %s to open (test-failed)', run.seed_id);

After store.updateRun(run.id, { status: 'conflict' }) at lines 203-204:
  await resetSeedToOpen(run.seed_id, this.projectPath);
  console.error('[refinery] Reset bead %s to open (conflict)', run.seed_id);

## Step 4 — Update syncBeadStatusOnStartup mapping

In run-status.ts, mapRunStatusToSeedStatus:
  - 'completed' should now map to 'review' (not 'closed') — pipeline done, pre-merge
  - 'merged' maps to 'closed' (unchanged)
  - 'test-failed', 'conflict' map to 'open' (unchanged)

Update terminalStatuses in syncBeadStatusOnStartup to include 'completed' mapping to 'review'.

## Step 5 — Tests

- finalize() with pushSucceeded=true: br update called with --status review (NOT br close)
- refinery mergeCompleted success path: closeSeed called after status=merged
- refinery test-failed path: resetSeedToOpen called after status=test-failed
- refinery conflict path: resetSeedToOpen called after status=conflict
- syncBeadStatusOnStartup: completed run → expectedSeedStatus is 'review' not 'closed'

## Dependencies

bd-0omb and bd-ytzv are subsets of this fix. Once bd-u5oq is implemented:
- bd-0omb (resetSeedToOpen after merge failure) is solved by Step 3
- bd-ytzv (push-failed still closes bead) is solved by Step 1 + Step 4 of bd-ytzv fix

title: finalize() in agent-worker.ts has no SessionLogs step — pipeline completion produces no session transcript
description: The finalize() function (agent-worker.ts:525) runs type-check, git add, git commit, git push, merge-queue enqueue, and br close. It writes FINALIZE_REPORT.md which captures build/commit/push/seed-close status, but no session log summarizing what the pipeline did (decisions, files changed, phase outcomes). title: finalize: push fails with 'src refspec does not match any' when worktree branch not checked out
description:
bd-vuk push failed with: error: src refspec foreman/bd-vuk does not match any

This means the worktree exists but the branch foreman/bd-vuk is not checked out in it. git push -u origin foreman/bd-vuk fails because there is no local ref to push.

Likely caused by worktree being in detached HEAD state or on a different branch. The finalize step does not verify the worktree is on the correct branch before attempting push.

Fix: add a pre-push check in finalize() to verify git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD == foreman/<seedId>, and if not, attempt git checkout foreman/<seedId> before pushing.

title: task-backend-ops: addLabelsToBead uses --labels flag which was renamed to --add-label in br
description:
addLabelsToBead() in src/orchestrator/task-backend-ops.ts builds the command:
  br update <seedId> --labels <label1>,<label2>,...

But br has renamed/changed this flag. Current br help shows:
  --add-label <ADD_LABEL>   Add label(s)
  --set-labels <SET_LABELS> Set label(s) (replaces all)

The --labels flag no longer exists and the command fails with:
  error: unexpected argument '--labels' found
  tip: a similar argument exists: '--set-labels'

This is a non-fatal warning (caught and logged) but means phase-tracking labels are never applied to beads.

Fix: change line 182 in task-backend-ops.ts from:
  const args = ["update", seedId, "--labels", labels.join(",")];
to:
  const args = ["update", seedId, "--add-label", ...labels];

title: foreman reset reopens already-closed beads when clearing failed runs
description: resetSeedToOpen() in task-backend-ops.ts is called unconditionally for every failed run during foreman reset. If the corresponding bead is already 'closed' (work completed successfully in a later run), the reset incorrectly calls 'br update <id> --status open', reopening a finished bead. Only call resetSeedToOpen() if bead status is NOT 'closed'. This check should live in the reset command handler (src/cli/commands/reset.ts) or in the Dispatcher.resetRuns() path.

title: foreman run exits when no beads ready instead of waiting for new work
description: foreman run dispatches available beads then exits if nothing is ready. It should run continuously (like a daemon) until explicitly stopped (Ctrl-C), polling for new beads to arrive. Use case: a user creates new beads mid-run (e.g. via 'br create' or foreman sling), or a bead becomes unblocked when a dependency closes — foreman should pick these up automatically without requiring a manual re-run. Fix: add a --watch-interval flag (default ~30s) that keeps the dispatch loop alive, sleeping between polls. When no beads are ready and no agents are active, print 'Waiting for work...' and sleep. Exit only on SIGINT/SIGTERM or --no-watch flag. The existing --no-watch flag should preserve current exit-immediately behavior for scripting/CI use.

title: foreman doctor deletes orphaned worktrees without verifying branch is on origin
description: doctor.ts:293-317 removes worktrees that have no matching run record ('orphaned' case). But a run record can be missing because: (a) run was purged from SQLite while worktree_path changed, or (b) run record was manually deleted. In either case the worktree may contain committed agent work whose branch is on origin but not yet merged to main. doctor --fix would delete the worktree, leaving an unmerged remote branch with no local context. Fix: before calling removeWorktree() in the 'no runs' case, check if the branch exists on origin (git ls-remote origin refs/heads/foreman/<seedId>). If it does, warn and skip rather than delete.

title: br sync --flush-only silently no-ops when dirty flag not set by CLI subprocess
description: When br close/update is called via execFileSync subprocess (as foreman does in task-backend-ops.ts), the SQLite dirty flag is apparently not set. As a result, 'br sync --flush-only' reports 'Nothing to export (no dirty issues)' even though the JSONL is stale. Only '--force' actually exports. Root cause is likely that the dirty flag is tracked per-connection or not written by the CLI close command path. Fix options: (1) foreman always uses '--force' in its sync calls, or (2) br fixes dirty flag persistence when closing via CLI. Short-term workaround: use 'br sync --flush-only --force' in foreman.

title: finalize() calls closeSeed() unconditionally even when git push fails
description: In agent-worker.ts finalize(), closeSeed() at line 623 is called unconditionally. If git push fails (pushSucceeded=false, line 578-589), the branch is not on origin, no merge will ever happen, but the bead is still closed. The branch is also not enqueued to the merge queue. Run status is set to 'completed' which is wrong — the branch never left the worktree. Fix: guard closeSeed() behind if (pushSucceeded). In the push-failed branch, call resetSeedToOpen() to leave the bead in a retryable state.

title: Single-agent worker mode does not reset bead to open on failure
description:
Pipeline mode calls markStuck() which calls resetSeedToOpen() so failed beads reappear in br ready for retry. Single-agent mode (non-pipeline) does not call markStuck() — it updates the SQLite run to failed/stuck directly without touching the bead. The bead remains in its current status (likely 'in_progress') rather than being updated.

The correct behavior mirrors pipeline mode:
- Transient error (rate limit) → reset to 'open' + comment
- Permanent failure → set to 'failed' + comment with error summary

Fix: single-agent failure paths in agent-worker.ts should call the same failure handling as markStuck() including bead status update and comment.

title: finalize: non-fast-forward push failure causes infinite sentinel retry loop
description:
When git push fails with 'non-fast-forward' during finalize, the run is marked stuck and resetSeedToOpen() resets the bead to open. The sentinel/dispatcher immediately re-dispatches, all phases are skipped (artifacts exist), push fails again, repeat. bd-qtqs accumulated 151 stuck runs in ~20 minutes from this loop.

Root cause: finalize() returns false on any push failure → runPipeline() calls resetSeedToOpen() unconditionally → bead goes open → dispatcher re-dispatches with no backoff.

Non-fast-forward is a deterministic failure (diverged history), not a transient error. Retrying without resolving the divergence will always fail.

Observed error:
  ! [rejected] foreman/bd-qtqs -> foreman/bd-qtqs (non-fast-forward)
  error: failed to push some refs

Local had 5 commits, origin had 1, branches had diverged.

Fix options:
1. Attempt git pull --rebase before push in finalize()
2. Classify non-fast-forward as 'conflict' status (not stuck) to skip resetSeedToOpen
3. Add exponential backoff in dispatcher before re-dispatching a seed that was recently stuck
